response.justification: Bogdan Szabo is a capable full-stack web and backend engineer, but his profile fundamentally mismatches this role's core requirements. His C++ exposure is limited to roughly six months at National Instruments (2014–2015) and a bachelor thesis project—far short of the 5–10 years of hands-on Windows C++ development required. There is no discernible experience in fixed income, fixed income derivatives, investment banking, or capital markets systems; his one fintech-adjacent role (RIDE Capital) involved Node.js/TypeScript in a startup context unrelated to trading systems. No SQL Server or Oracle depth is demonstrated, and desirable skills like Summit, Kafka, MQ, or Python are absent.
strengths: Demonstrated CI/CD and DevOps experience (GitLab, Docker, Artifactory) across multiple roles
Strong communication skills evidenced by conference talks, workshops, and community teaching
Java experience listed, partially satisfying one optional skill
concerns: C++ experience is minimal (~6 months) and not on Windows-platform production systems—nowhere near the 5–10 year requirement
Zero capital markets, fixed income, or investment banking domain exposure
No SQL Server or Oracle experience; primary database background is MongoDB and MySQL in web contexts

key_factors.factor: No capital markets / fixed income domain knowledge
